title: Tapping for the Event I Can't Release
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the event that stays caught, years after
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the event that stays caught in the body years after it happened. The script does not erase the event. It does meet the body's stuckness around it so the next small step is possible.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The tapping shape is EFT. EFT was made by Gary Craig in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when an event from the past still has a strong charge in the body. Do as a stand-alone practice or alongside therapy or other long-term work on the event.
practice.whenNotToUse: Not for an acute trauma in its first weeks. In that period a slower, supported approach is more workable.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the event that stays caught in the body. The script does not erase the event. It meets the stuckness so the next small step is possible.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though this event is still alive in my body and I have not been able to let it go, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I have tried to release this and have not been able to, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though letting it go does not have to happen all at once, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** This event is still here.
**Side of eye:** I thought I was done with it.
**Under eye:** It still catches me when I am not expecting it.
**Under nose:** I am tired of carrying it.
**Chin:** It changed my life.
**Collarbone:** Part of me will not put it down.
**Under arm:** Part of me is afraid to.
**Top of head:** It does not have to leave today.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** This event is still here, and I can be on my own side about that.
**Side of eye:** The body has been doing its best to keep me safe.
**Under eye:** I can let it move at the pace it can move.
**Under nose:** I have lived with this. I will keep living.
**Chin:** It changed my life, and my life is still mine.
**Collarbone:** I do not have to forgive on a schedule.
**Under arm:** I can soften my grip on it a little today.
**Top of head:** I can keep coming back to this until it loosens.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. The tapping shape is EFT. EFT was made by Gary Craig in the mid-1990s.
